channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Word of the Day: oligarchy
item.description: This word has appeared in 105 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: ¿El estadio más grande del mundo puede explicar la economía global?
item.description: Las altas tasas de crecimiento y el auge de las exportaciones en Vietnam ocultan profundas frustraciones en uno de los países más ambiciosos de Asia.
item.dc:creator: Damien Cave and Linh Pham
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Trabajadores frente a las obras del estadio Hung Vuong, en Hanói, Vietnam, en mayo.

item.title: What to Know About Getting Around Town on Swift’s Wedding Day
item.description: With Taylor Swift’s wedding on Friday, Midtown Manhattan will present some logistical challenges to drivers and pedestrians.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy
item.category: Moynihan Train Hall at Pennsylvania Station (Manhattan, NY)

media:content.media:description: Madison Square Garden is expected to be the site of the pop star’s wedding celebration.

item.title: What Does It Cost to Take Over Madison Square Garden?
item.description: It’s not just rent. There’s security, insurance, permits and other costs, not to mention extra police presence.
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta, Ishani Desai, Maria Cramer and Maia Coleman

media:content.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Preparations at Madison Square Garden on Thursday.

item.title: How Public Pools Create Summer Communities in New York
item.description: Dry Dock Pool in Lower Manhattan is one of New York City’s 51 free outdoor pools where more than one million New Yorkers cool off with swimming, sunblock and community.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Hu and Lexi Parra

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for July 3, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Tibetan Activist Sets Self on Fire Outside U.N. in Protest Against China
item.description: Lobga Rangzen, a 52-year-old resident of Queens, died after the self-immolation. He said Beijing’s policies were “destroying the Tibetan people.”
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:description: A Tibetan activist set himself on fire outside the U.N. headquarters in New York on Thursday evening. “I am doing this for my country. For the Tibetan nation,” he had said.

item.title: ¿Es necesario un entrenamiento especial para la menopausia?
item.description: La menopausia se ha convertido en un tema habitual en la cultura popular, y el entrenamiento de fuerza se ha erigido en una de las recomendaciones más recurrentes para un envejecimiento saludable.
item.dc:creator: Liz Baker Plosser

media:content.media:credit: Frances F. Denny para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Liz Birenbaum, de 60 años, hace sesiones de fuerza de 30 minutos dos veces por semana con un programa de entrenamiento para la mediana edad.

item.title: ‘Basura’: Gloria y Emily Estefan encuentran un nuevo ritmo en el teatro
item.description: Madre e hija pasaron años componiendo las canciones y partitura de “Basura”, su nuevo musical. “En toda relación entre madre e hija, las cosas son complicadas”, dijo Gloria Estefan.
item.dc:creator: David Peisner

item.title: EE. UU. cumple 250 años: esta es la polémica por las celebraciones
item.description: Dos organizaciones nacionales, una de ellas respaldada por el presidente Donald Trump, han organizado un conjunto de actividades para celebrar. Los estados también están organizando numerosos eventos.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra and Jennifer Schuessler

media:content.media:description: Un trabajador sube a una rueda de la fortuna en construcción para la Great American State Fair de Freedom 250, que comenzó la semana pasada.

item.title: Record Heat to Persist Through July 4th Weekend
item.description: Triple-digit temperatures were forecast for large swaths of the United States as the nation prepared its 250th Independence Day celebrations.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:description: Scorching temperatures will continue through the weekend. Most areas will cool a few degrees by Sunday.

item.title: Denmark Has a Big Fourth of July Party. This Year, the U.S. Is Uninvited.
item.description: Furious at President Trump over Greenland, Danish officials took the unprecedented step of pressuring organizers to cut American officials from the program.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Gettleman and Maya Tekeli

media:content.media:credit: Charlotte de la Fuente for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Festival volunteers show the American flag that will be raised on July 4 despite the absence of American officials this year.

item.title: Ucrania intensifica la presión sobre Putin con ataques en Crimea
item.description: Los bombardeos con drones, los problemas de abastecimiento y los apagones en la península anexada por Rusia buscan aumentar el costo de la guerra para el Kremlin.
item.dc:creator: Paul Sonne and Nataliya Vasilyeva

media:content.media:description: Yevpatoriya, Crimea, el mes pasado. La región del mar Negro, que Rusia se anexionó a Ucrania en 2014, se ha convertido en uno de los principales focos de tensión de la guerra.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Crypto Windfall for Trump but Not for Most Investors
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Friday, July 3, 2026.

item.title: What We Found After He Was Gone
item.description: Jeff was Billy’s friend and my lover. Was he also Cupid?
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Laura Nelson

item.title: A Relationship That Forced Two Magicians to Tip Their Hand
item.description: For Jamie Allan and Natalie Villalonga-Stanton, the daring feats they performed reinforced their mutual reliance and trust.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Goodman

item.title: On Military Leave Long Enough to Get Married
item.description: After graduating from the U.S. Naval Academy, Emma Shattuck and Anders Gulbrandson dated face-to-face about 22 percent of the time.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

item.title: He Wrote Hollywood Love Stories. It Took 12 Years to Finish His Own.
item.description: Michael Elliot, a screenwriter whose rom-com hits include “Brown Sugar” and “Just Wright,” married Adia May, a lawyer, more than a decade after they first met at a Los Angeles airport.
item.dc:creator: Charreah K. Jackson

item.title: By the Fourth Date, They Created a Shared Google Calendar
item.description: After several dead-end dates, Barry Pump had resigned himself to remaining a bachelor, but then he met Shreyans Goenka and bonded over their shared penchant for meticulous planning.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: ‘All Different, but All American’: A Chef on Potato Salad Diversity
item.description: Wesley Morris, host of “Cannonball,” talks to the chef Hooni Kim on the streets of New York City about how he has made potato salad his own.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Fralin, Andrew Smith and Felice Leon

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for July 03, 2026
item.description: Barbara Lin returns to open our solving weekend.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:description: In skiing, unlike in crosswords, going down is much easier than going across.

item.title: Wesley Morris Makes the People’s Potato Salad
item.description: Wesley Morris, host of “Cannonball,” heads to the NYT Cooking kitchen to make his version of the great American dish: potato salad.
item.dc:creator: Gavy Contreras, Lauren Pruitt, Luke Piotrowski, Srdjan Stojiljkovic and Felice Leon

item.title: On Eve of Taylor Swift’s Wedding, Rehearsal Dinner Kicks Off Weekend of Events
item.description: The event for about 100 guests at Madison Square Garden kicked off a weekend of celebration for the pop star and the athlete, as celebrities arrived in town and reinforcements were delivered.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher

media:content.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Tent structures were put in place, security was beefed up, and streets were closed as the couple prepared for their mega celebrations.

item.title: Is Black Potato Salad a Standard Recipe?
item.description: On “Cannonball,” Wesley Morris talked to Jessica B. Harris, a two-time James Beard Award winner and food historian, about potato salad and how tastes developed in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Fralin, Alfredo Chiarappa, Lauren Pruitt and Felice Leon

item.title: Heat Wave Relief May Not Come at Sunset
item.description: There are steps you can take to cool down after sundown even in conditions with high humidity.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff and Dani Blum

media:content.media:description: A vendor pulls out a bottle of water from a tank using dry ice to keep it cold, during a heat wave on Thursday at the Great American State Fair on the National Mall in Washington.

item.title: U.K. Lawmakers Demand Deportation of ‘Grooming Gang’ Leader
item.description: Shabir Ahmed was handed a lengthy prison sentence in 2012 for his ties to a sexual exploitation ring.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin

media:content.media:description: Protesters demonstrating last month in Southampton, England, where a man had been sentenced the day before for a fatal stabbing in December. Some right-wing figures have tried to link immigration and crime.

item.title: Trump’s Huge Windfall Has Few Known Global Precedents
item.description: President Trump’s earnings in office are at a level once unimaginable for any leader of a liberal democracy, particularly a sitting American president.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump disclosed that he has made immense profits since returning to the presidency.

item.title: As Ukraine War Escalates, Witkoff and Kushner Are Focused on Iran
item.description: Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are pivotal players at a moment when the posts of U.S. ambassador to Moscow and Kyiv are both vacant.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Steve Witkoff, a White House special envoy, has met with President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia seven times since President Trump returned to office.

item.title: MAGA Base Stays Quiet After Trump Reports Billions in Personal Gains
item.description: A new mandatory disclosure revealed that the president has earned $2.2 billion during the first year back in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Emily Davies

media:content.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Some supporters of President Trump described his earnings as a validation of the business acumen they have long admired in him.

item.title: Rare Copy of the Declaration of Independence Is Discovered in London
item.description: A volunteer cataloging naval letters came across a copy of the document, printed 250 years ago in New Hampshire. “It was a thrilling find,” he said.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai

item.title: Animals Need to Cool Off, Too
item.description: Mammals across the Midwest are doing their best to stay cool during the heat wave, with some help from humans.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Williams, Dan Simmons and Jacey Fortin

media:content.media:credit: Madeleine Hordinski for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Elephants cooled off in their pool at the Cincinnati Zoo on Thursday.

item.title: Clusters of Severe Stomach Illness Reported Across the U.S.
item.description: Public health experts said there were more cases than usual of the disease caused by cyclospora, which is spread through contaminated food and drink.
item.dc:creator: Roni Rabin
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

item.title: Mamdani Is a Taylor Swift Fan. Here Are His Top 12 Songs.
item.description: Ahead of Taylor Swift’s wedding celebration in Manhattan, Mayor Zohran Mamdani revealed that he is a bigger fan of her music than previously known.
item.dc:creator: Emma G. Fitzsimmons

media:content.media:description: The wedding celebration of Taylor Swift and her fiancé Travis Kelce is set to take place on Friday at Madison Square Garden.

item.title: Louisiana Attorney General Charged With Bullying New Orleans Officials
item.description: Liz Murrill, a Republican, was indicted on charges of malfeasance and intimidation after sending warning letters to the city’s Democratic leaders over actions they took.
item.dc:creator: Rick Rojas

media:content.media:description: Attorney General Liz Murrill had warned municipal officials of “the possible forfeiture of your own office.”

item.title: UPS Did Not Tell Inspectors to Check Failed Part in Louisville Crash
item.description: Inspectors were not instructed to check a bearing that they had been told was faulty, but the shipping company says Boeing, the plane’s manufacturer, is to blame.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian

media:content.media:description: An MD-11 cargo jet operated by UPS crashed shortly after takeoff at the Louisville Muhammad Ali International Airport in November.

item.title: Ocho días bajo los escombros: el rescate que emocionó a Venezuela
item.description: Hernán Gil, de 44 años, fue rescatado con vida de los escombros de un edificio colapsado, en un momento de alivio entre la devastación.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Sulbarán, Shawn Hubler and Genevieve Glatsky

media:content.media:description: Rescue workers attend to Hernán Alberto Gil Flores after he was pulled from the rubble eight days after he was trapped by twin earthquakes that struck Catia La Mar, Venezuela, on Thursday.

item.title: Texas Governor Appoints Former Hard Right Critic to Key Post
item.description: Gov. Greg Abbott appointed a firebrand former opponent, Don Huffines, to be state comptroller, underscoring a G.O.P. push for unity ahead of the midterms.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman

media:content.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Don Huffines at a forum featuring gubernatorial candidates running to unseat Gov. Greg Abbott in 2022.

item.title: A Sweltering Fourth of July
item.description: Also, Taylor’s wedding. Here’s the latest at the end of Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Evan Gorelick

media:content.media:credit: Carolina Herrera for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Chinatown in Manhattan today.

item.title: New York Gallery Surrenders 20 Artifacts Thought Looted From Cambodia
item.description: The Madison Avenue business turned over stone statues and other items that it had purchased from an art dealer accused of trafficking in illicit objects.
item.dc:creator: Tom Mashberg and Graham Bowley

item.title: Deadly MV Hondius Hantavirus Outbreak Is Over, W.H.O. Says
item.description: The rodent-borne virus, which sickened passengers on the MV Hondius, killed three people, led to a race to find its origin and a global health alert.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan

media:content.media:description: The MV Hondius had only a couple of dozen passengers and crew members as it left the port in Tenerife, one of the Canary Islands, on May 11. It began its journey six weeks earlier in Argentina with about 150 passengers.

item.title: 8 Days After Venezuela Earthquakes, Rescuers Pull a Man From the Rubble
item.description: Rescuers pulled 44-year-old Hernán Gil from a collapsed basement on Thursday, eight days after earthquakes hit Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

media:content.media:description: Rescue workers attend to Hernán Alberto Gil Flores after he was pulled from the rubble eight days after he was trapped by twin earthquakes that struck Catia La Mar, Venezuela, on Thursday.

item.title: The Most Iconic American Artwork Is the Hardest to See
item.description: The Statue of Liberty is caught between the sublime and the souvenir, our critic Jason Farago explains. Look again: She’s much stranger than you think.
item.dc:creator: Jason Farago

item.title: Egg Producers to Pay $3.3 Million to Settle Federal Price-Fixing Lawsuit
item.description: The companies also agreed to donate more than 53 million eggs to food banks and nonprofits in 17 states that joined the federal government in the antitrust action.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

media:content.media:description: The federal government began its investigation as the average wholesale price of a dozen eggs reached a high above $6 in March 2025.

item.title: F.B.I. Assigns Scores of Analysts to Examine Election Records in Georgia
item.description: The expansion of an investigation into Fulton County’s election office reflects President Trump’s desire to prove his baseless claims that the 2020 election in Georgia was rigged.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti, Alan Feuer, Dustin Volz and Richard Fausset

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A memo says that the surge is part of a priority effort by Kash Patel, the director of the F.B.I.

item.title: How the Mood in Little Senegal Went From Euphoria to Despair
item.description: At watch parties in Upper Manhattan, joyous fans were stunned by a sudden twist in the match between Belgium and the underdog Senegal.
item.dc:creator: Corey Kilgannon

media:content.media:description: When Senegal scored its second goal, the mood was joyful among fans in Harlem.

item.title: Judge Demands Answers About Plans for Trump’s East Potomac Golf Course
item.description: In a fiery hearing, Judge Ana C. Reyes hammered the government over denials that the president was forging ahead with plans to renovate the course without approval.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague
item.category: White House East Wing and Ballroom (Washington, DC)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump inspected plans for his proposed renovation of the East Potomac Golf Course on Sunday.

item.title: Tesla Driver Using Autopilot in Texas Crash Is Charged With Manslaughter
item.description: A front-door video camera of the crash, which killed a woman inside her home, showed the Tesla plowing into a house through its driveway.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra E. Petri

media:content.media:description: A Tesla Model 3 using the Full Self Driving Beta software in 2023.

item.title: Heat Wave Updates: New York Hits 100 Degrees as High Temperatures Stifle Eastern U.S.
item.description: The temperature reading in Central Park tied a record for July 2 set in 1966. Some 163 million people, from Missouri to Maine and south to Mississippi, live in areas that are likely to experience dangerous heat on Thursday, forecasters said.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Lanna Apisukh for The New York Times

item.title: World Cup Updates: Portugal-Croatia Helps the World Cup Say Farewell to Toronto
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
